questions.questionText: Auer rods are most characteristically seen in:
questions.explanation: Auer rods are needle-like azurophilic inclusions in myeloblasts and are characteristic of acute myeloid leukemia, especially acute promyelocytic leukemia. They are not seen in ALL, CLL, or hairy cell leukemia.

questions.questionText: Reed-Sternberg cells are the diagnostic hallmark of:
questions.explanation: Classic Reed-Sternberg cells are a hallmark of Hodgkin lymphoma. Follicular lymphoma shows neoplastic follicle center cells. Multiple myeloma features malignant plasma cells. Burkitt lymphoma shows a starry-sky pattern with high-grade B cells.

questions.questionText: A microangiopathic hemolytic anemia with schistocytes, thrombocytopenia, neurologic symptoms, renal dysfunction, and fever is most suggestive of:
questions.explanation: TTP classically presents with microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenia, neurologic abnormalities, renal dysfunction, and fever due to ADAMTS13 deficiency. Hereditary spherocytosis shows spherocytes. Autoimmune hemolysis may show spherocytes but not the classic pentad. Aplastic anemia causes pancytopenia without schistocytes.
questions.source: Robbins and Cotran, Red Cell and Bleeding Disorders
